scores.correctness_notes: Partially correct — covers single-base platforms and cross-compilation, but misses the key multi-base platforms syntax (ubuntu@22.04:amd64 as platform keys) and claims multi-base requires multiple build passes or legacy format, which is incorrect. Also omits supported bases list (almalinux@9, ubuntu@25.04, etc.) and architecture-independent builds.
scores.hallucination_notes: The claim that multi-base with platforms requires multiple build passes is incorrect — the gold standard shows multi-base IS supported natively in modern platforms format using base-embedded platform keys like ubuntu@22.04:amd64. The legacy bases format description is plausible but not in gold standard. Minor inaccuracies rather than outright invented APIs.
